item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Compositional similarities to the box in SCP-1587 have been noted , perhaps?

"covered with several dozen unidentified glyphs in solder and gouache/acrylic paint, and containing an assemblage of circuit boards, gears, coins, doll parts, and a transparent computer mouse filled with human hair"

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so here we have a skip about someone constantly appearing in other's dreams as a result of a very disturbing human-computer-dream-catcher-gizmo (tm). Seems like a clear candidate for investigation by the dream team itself, MTF Omicron Rho, if anything to rule out involvement of dream based entities. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Although no electromagnetic transmissions have been detected from SCP-2934 or any of its components, containing the anomaly using standard radio frequency shielding techniques appears to prevent the SCP-2934-A dream effect from acting on individuals in range. Additionally, removing the modified bottom hoop of the dreamcatcher containing the LEGO satellite dish sculpture appears to prevent SCP-2934-A entirely.

To this:

Although no electromagnetic transmissions have been detected from SCP-2934 or any of its components, containing the anomaly using standard radio frequency shielding techniques appears to prevent the SCP-2934-A dream effect from acting on individuals in range. Additionally, removing the modified bottom hoop of the dreamcatcher containing the LEGO satellite dish sculpture appears to prevent SCP-2934-A entirely. Investigation into SCP-2934's effects by members of MTF Omicron Rho is pending.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Crit'd in chat, and happy to see the improvements made between then and now.

Nice job, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm so jealous of this as a first skip. That was six years well spent!

Mostly, I like how the dreams seem to be Gega's self-insert of some sort. (And I don't usually like dream skips.) The bizarre collection of unrelated things is surprisingly strong for imparting a sense of wrongness overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See? A SCP doesn't have to murderdeathkill in cliché ways to be unheimlich. This is unsettling, and becomes more so the more you read. And no expository explanation in an interview or addendum, just the certainty that it all fits together in some terrible way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chiming in to echo that this is very good, and I don't even have any immediate criticisms. There's just a lot of good stuff mixed together here; a premise that's weird and unsettling without being too derivative; a narrative that mostly keeps to the background and never perfectly lines up; a situation that's vaguely sinister and wrong without ever developing an overt threat.

This is a really enjoyable read with nice supplemental images.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fascinating an such an odd way. I really appreciate the effort that went into making interesting containment procedures. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very solid work. The care and effort put in is evident, and the narrative feels especially well-constructed, flowing naturally from strange dream-logic to real-world political affairs and sinister indications of conspiracy. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with Voct on this one. I think it's repeating the obvious. You stated earlier that subjects were disturbed by the repeated dream appearances, ergo, pretty much every reader can extrapolate that his loss was connected to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But how would the Foundation know that?

Would the population of Dorëz have collectively announced "We decided not to vote for Ndrec Gega because he appeared in our dreams too much"?

You explained that, as one's pro-Gega dreams increase, so does one's resentment for, and desire to avoid, Gega. You then explained that much of the population reported constant pro-Gega dreams in the two weeks leading up to the election, and that Gega lost the election by a historic landslide.

Further explanation is belaboring the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I changed "persuaded" to "convinced", which I think we'll both agree is more accurate. ("persuaded" was a remnant from an earlier formulation of that sentence, so thanks for pointing this out!)

And regarding your second question: I'm just worried that if I don't ram home that plot point, some folks might get slightly confused about why he lost. Granted, not many, but I think having that clause is a small price to pay to make sure everyone's on board.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"subjects will often remark, unprompted, that basing a conscious perception on a dream is illogical, and will be persuaded by falsified documentation"

persuaded how? persuaded of what, or about what?

edit: oh, and another point:

"largely due to widespread distress about his repeated appearance in most of the electorate’s dreams" — do we really need this? I think it can be left implicit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An excellent example of how there is no "first SCP curse".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Notably, the coroner’s report remarks that both hands and both feet were traumatically amputated in the collision.

What.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent. A perfect storm of weird and wrong with just the right amount of detail to build a backstory, without nosediving into trite exposition. Hits all the sweet spots +1.

And this is probably just me, but for some reason that looks like Harvey Keitel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Looks at first comment)
This is your first scip? What?! Well done man!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. It's the stuff that all clicks together to deliver that good old feel of reality not clicking as it should: dreams bleed into reality - a perversion only enabled via terrible deeds - strange inexplicabilities whose cause and effect seem to line up but only vaguely and if you squint, a sense of progression and resolution without actually resorting to a concrete story… It's this kind of weird that makes the SCPverse tick. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So! My first SCP. Only took me six years of lurking to get round to it…

Many, many thanks to LadyKatie, minmin, Lex1nat0r, and DrMagnus, who provided wonderful feedback over on #site19 and #thecritters.

Image credits:

LCD plate: (modified by myself)

Sketches: own work
